title: Sisyrinchium intihuatanense
taxonomy: Sisyrinchium intihuatanense is classified as a species within the genus Sisyrinchium, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as blue-eyed grasses or ragged sword grasses. This classification places it within the family Iridaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants that includes irises and other similar species. Sisyrinchium intihuatanense is also often classified as belonging to the subfamily Sisyrinchieae, which is a subfamily within the family Iridaceae. At the rank of kingdom, it is classified as a flowering plant, or angiosperm, and at the rank of domain, it is classified as a eukaryote. The full classification of Sisyrinchium intihuatanense is as follows: kingdom Plantae, domain Eukarya, kingdom Angiosperm, family Iridaceae, subfamily Sisyrinchieae, genus Sisyrinchium, species S. intihuatanense.

intro: Sisyrinchium intihuatanense, also known as the Yellow Eyebane or False Blue-Eyed Grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Iridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Sisyrinchium intihuatanense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
